rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
generic_rules: Use type-safe expressions in AGREE: align helper declaration types with actual expression types.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Write deterministic dependency guarantees that relate visible inputs to visible outputs.
Omit unsafe clauses when Boolean data is opaque or used in numeric comparisons.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Use `eq` for defining helper variables in AGREE with correct type alignment.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not generate range assumptions or guarantees without concrete bounds or visible feature names.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
